summary: Laid‑back Red Rocks show shines on the first Miracle and a soaring “If I Had The World To Give”, but the audience tape is only fair in clarity.
review: Fans rave about the unique, relaxed vibe of this Red Rocks performance – especially the debut Miracle jam, the heartfelt “World To Give”/“If I Had The World To Give” sequence and a monster Sugaree.  The recording is an audience (AUD) capture; while the second‑set soundboard portions are praised, overall the tape suffers from venue acoustics, chatter and occasional muddiness that can make listening a test of patience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers love the musical moments (high show weight) but note significant recording flaws; the mixed star scores and divergent comments lead to a balanced rating with medium confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Chatter does not completely obscure music, but venue acoustics cause sound to dissipate; some parts (2nd‑set) are from a soundboard and sound clearer, while the overall tape is not clean and can feel muddy.

standout_songs: If I Had The World To Give
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as laid‑back and unrushed, with a relaxed second set that weaves jams together (Estimated > Other One > Eyes) and a smooth transition into an upbeat “Iko” feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocals on “If I Had The World To Give” are highlighted along with several cool guitar licks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned for cosmic vocalizing during the jam and overall bass/drum space feeling.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s slide licks receive praise in the later jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work in the final jam (Aiko Aiko) is singled out as a favorite moment.
